heroHeadline: El Mayo Sentencing Postponed to May 18 — Escuinapa Police Force Collapses After 5 Officers Killed in 9 Days
heroSubtitle: 625 days since Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's capture — EDNY sentencing postponed a second time (to May 18, 2026); defense cited ongoing Sinaloa violence blocking safe witness access. Escuinapa police in institutional collapse: 5 officers killed in 9 days (Mar 31–Apr 8), 32 officers (~40% of force) resigned; Governor Rocha Moya deployed 1,190 SEDENA/GN troops. April 10: drive-by on Boulevard Madero kills 2 in Culiacán. 2,415+ conflict homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. CJNG led by Juan Carlos Valencia González (El Mencho's stepson, confirmed El País Apr 6); Chapitos-CJNG alliance uncertain under new leadership. La Mayiza controls ~90% of former Chapitos territory; civil war at 579 days with no ceasefire.
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae, La Jornada, CBC News, Excelsior, El Diario del Noroeste, White House ONDCP, DOJ, El Informador, El Imparcial, Latin Times, Zeta Tijuana
